title: <div>  3.  Ten (Twist) – He meets Takuan, a monk who challenges his way of think
content:
3. Ten (Twist) – He meets Takuan, a monk who challenges his way of thinking. Strength is not about winning—it’s about mastering oneself.
4. Ketsu (Conclusion) – Musashi’s real battle is not with others, but with his own arrogance. His journey is about learning, not fighting.

SECTION 2: GENERATING YOUR STORY (FROM LOGLINE TO EXPANDED STRUCTURE)

Step 1: Write Your Logline

A logline is a one-sentence summary of your story that captures its core conflict.

Here are five real logline examples from film and manga:
1. A fugitive samurai roams Japan seeking the true meaning of strength, only to realize his greatest enemy is himself. (Vagabond)
2. A young jazz drummer joins an elite music program, only to be pushed beyond his limits by an abusive mentor. (Whiplash)
3. A boy bullies a deaf girl in school, then years later tries to make amends for his past cruelty. (A Silent Voice)
4. A troubled teen discovers a passion for painting, but finds that the pursuit of art demands everything from him. (Blue Period)
